The '''77th British Academy Film Awards''', more commonly known as the [[British Academy Film Awards|BAFTAs]], were held on 18 February 2024, honouring the best national and foreign films of [[2023 in film|2023]], at the [[Royal Festival Hall]] within [[London]]'s [[Southbank Centre]]. Presented by the [[British Academy of Film and Television Arts]], accolades were handed out for the best feature-length film and documentaries of any nationality that were screened at British cinemas in 2023.{{efn|Attributed to multiple sources: <ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2023/05/bafta-2024-film-awards-date-1235383365/ |title=BAFTA Sets 2024 Film Awards Date |last=Ntim |first=Zac The ceremony was hosted by [[David Tennant]] for the first time; "I am delighted to have been asked to host the EE BAFTA Film Awards and help celebrate the very best of this year's films and the many brilliant people who bring them to life", said Tennant.{{efn|Attributed to multiple sources: <ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/awards/global/david-tennant-host-bafta-film-awards-1235861782/ |title=David Tennant to Host BAFTA Film Awards |last=Ritman |first=Alex |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=4 January 2024 |access-date=5 January 2024 |archive-date=5 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240105000125/https://variety.com/2024/awards/global/david-tennant-host-bafta-film-awards-1235861782/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.standard.co.uk/showbiz/david-tennant-southbank-centre-catherine-tate-scottish-london-b1138650.html |title=David Tennant says BAFTA Film Awards will be 'evening of generosity and joy' |last=Clarke |first=Naomi |work=[[The Standard (London newspaper)|The Standard]] |date=12 February 2024 |access-date=13 February 2024 |archive-date=12 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240212142114/https://www.standard.co.uk/showbiz/david-tennant-southbank-centre-catherine-tate-scottish-london-b1138650.html |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.independent.co.uk/news/david-tennant-ap-bradley-cooper-london-martin-scorsese-b2495474.html |title=One actor won't be nervous at the BAFTA Film Awards. |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240215000025/https://www.independent.co.uk/news/david-tennant-ap-bradley-cooper-london-martin-scorsese-b2495474.html |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/david-tennant-bafta-host-taylor-swift-1235906980/ |title=David Tennant on How the Golden Globes Informed His BAFTAs Hosting Stint: 'Don't Diss' Taylor Swift |url=https://ew.com/david-tennant-michael-sheen-tom-hiddleston-bafta-2024-monologue-8584712 |title=David Tennant reunites with Michael Sheen during BAFTA monologue to find a dog sitter for Bark [[BBC One]] and [[BBC iPlayer|iPlayer]] in the UK from 19:00–21:00 [[Greenwich Mean Time|GMT]], and on [[BritBox|BritBox International]] in Canada, Denmark, Finland, Norway, South Africa, Sweden and the US.<ref name="Attendees">{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/ee-bafta-film-awards-2024-attendees-announced |title=Nominees and special guests unveiled ahead The BAFTA longlists were unveiled on 5 January 2024.<ref name="Longlists">{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/longlists-2024-ee-bafta-film-awards |title=BAFTA announces Longlists for the 2024 EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=5 January 2024 |access-date=5 January 2024 |archive-date=20 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240120012544/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/longlists-2024-ee-bafta-film-awards |url-status=live}}</ref> The nominations were announced via [[Live streaming|livestream]] by former [[BAFTA Rising Star Award|EE Rising Star Award]] nominees [[Naomi Ackie]] and [[Kingsley Ben-Adir]], from the arts charity's HQ at 195 Piccadilly, London, on 18 January 2024; the livestream was also available to watch on BAFTA's [[Twitter]] and [[YouTube]] pages.{{efn|Attributed to multiple sources: <ref name="Key Dates"/><ref name="Longlists"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2024/01/how-to-watch-2024-bafta-awards-nomination-livestream-1235795247/ |title=BAFTA Awards Nominations: Watch the Livestream Here January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240118122604/https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-68016520 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref name="Nominations">{{cite web |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240119130711/https://deadline.com/2024/01/bafta-awards-nominations-list-2024-1235796022/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite AV media |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/awards/bafta-rising-star-jacob-elordi-ayo-edebiri-phoebe-dynevor-1235867319/ |title=Jacob Elordi, Ayo Edebiri, Phoebe Dynevor were announced on 18 February 2024.<ref name="Key Dates"/>

The [[Epic film|epic]] [[biographical film|biographical]] [[thriller film|thriller]] film ''[[Oppenheimer (film)|Oppenheimer]]'' received the most nominations with thirteen and the most wins with seven, including [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]], [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] ([[Christopher Nolan]]) and [[BAFTA Award for Best Actor in a Leading Role|Best Actor]] ([[Cillian Murphy]]); British films ''[[Poor Things (film)|Poor Things]]'' and ''[[The Zone of Interest (film)|The Zone of Interest]]'' also won multiple awards.{{efn|Attributed to multiple sources: <ref name="Winners"/><ref name="Deadline"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/awards/bafta-film-awards-2024-winners-list-1235913491/ |title=BAFTA Awards: 'Oppenheimer' and 'Poor Things' Win Big as 'Barbie' and 'Maestro' Are Shut Out — Full Winners List |last1=Yossman |first1=K.J. |last2=Shafer |first2=Ellise |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=18 February 2024 |access-date=19 February 2024 |archive-date=18 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240218173615/https://variety.com/2024/film/awards/bafta-film-awards-2024-winners-list-1235913491/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://greekherald.com.au/news/yorgos-lanthimos-film-poor-things-scoops-multiple-awards-at-the-baftas/ |title=Yorgos Lanthimos' film 'Poor Things' scoops multiple awards at the BAFTAs |work=[[The Greek Herald]] |date=19 February 2024 |access-date=19 February 2024 |archive-date=20 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240220053558/https://greekherald.com.au/news/yorgos-lanthimos-film-poor-things-scoops-multiple-awards-at-the-baftas/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.polskieradio.pl/395/7991/artykul/3337188,polishbritishus-film-zone-of-interest%C2%A0wins%C2%A0three-bafta-awards |title=Polish-British-US film 'Zone of Interest' wins three BAFTA Awards |publisher=[[Radio Poland]] |date=19 February 2024 |access-date=19 February 2024 |archive-date=26 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240226175222/https://www.polskieradio.pl/395/7991/Artykul/3337188%2CPolishBritishUS-film-Zone-of-Interest%C2%A0wins%C2%A0three-BAFTA-awards |url-status=live}}</ref>}}

The BAFTA longlists were announced on 5 January 2024.<ref name="Longlists"/> The nominees were announced on 18 January 2024.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2024-ee-bafta-film-awards-nominations-announced |title=Nominations Announced for the 2024 EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=18 January 2024 |access-date=18 January 2024 |archive-date=1 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240201093428/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/2024-ee-bafta-film-awards-nominations-announced |url-status=live}}</ref> The winners were announced on 18 February 2024.<ref>{{cite press release |url=https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/winners-announced-ee-bafta-film-awards |title=Winners Announced: EE BAFTA Film Awards |publisher=[[British Academy of Film and Television Arts|BAFTA]] |date=18 February 2024 |access-date=18 February 2024 |archive-date=19 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240219041613/https://www.bafta.org/media-centre/press-releases/winners-announced-ee-bafta-film-awards |url-status=live}}</ref>

==Ceremony information==
[[File:Royal Festival Hall (37366103611).jpg|thumb|upright=2.0|right|The interior of the [[Royal Festival Hall]], which hosted the ceremony]]

The broadcast was streamed live on [[BBC One]] and [[BBC iPlayer|iPlayer]] in the United Kingdom, and on [[BritBox|BritBox International]] in Canada, Denmark, Finland, Norway, South Africa, Sweden and the United States; due to the significant time difference, Australia-based BritBox subscribers were able to watch the awards on-demand.<ref name="Attendees"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/mediacentre/articles/2024/bafta-film-awards-how-to-watch |title=BAFTA Film Awards 2024 – How to Watch on TV and BBC iPlayer |publisher=[[BBC]] |date=18 February 2024 |access-date=18 February 2024 |archive-date=16 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240216161602/https://www.bbc.com/mediacentre/articles/2024/bafta-film-awards-how-to-watch |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/tv/global/britbox-bafta-film-tv-awards-streaming-1235902004/ |title=BritBox Inks New Deal to Stream BAFTA Film, TV Awards Internationally Through 2025 (Exclusive) |last=Yossman |first=K.J. |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=8 February 2024 |access-date=11 February 2024 |archive-date=9 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240209032919/https://variety.com/2024/tv/global/britbox-bafta-film-tv-awards-streaming-1235902004/ |url-status=live}}</ref> The red carpet was hosted by [[Clara Amfo]] and [[Alex Zane]], live on BAFTA's social channels [[Facebook]], [[Twitter|X]] and [[YouTube]], while Zainab Jiwa was live from the red carpet exclusively on BAFTA's [[TikTok]] channel from 15:00–16:00 [[Greenwich Mean Time|GMT]].<ref name="Attendees"/>

The trio of ''[[Barbie (film)|Barbie]]'', ''[[Killers of the Flower Moon (film)|Killers of the Flower Moon]]'', and ''[[Oppenheimer (film)|Oppenheimer]]'' led the longlists, tying with fifteen nods each; this haul of fifteen longlist mentions equalled the record held by the previous year's ''[[All Quiet on the Western Front (2022 film)|All Quiet on the Western Front]]''. ''[[Poor Things (film)|Poor Things]]'' and ''[[Maestro (2023 film)|Maestro]]'' followed with fourteen and twelve, respectively.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2024/01/bafta-longlist-oppenheimer-barbie-killers-of-flower-moon-maestro-christopher-nolan-greta-gerwig-martin-scorsese-1235695719/ |title=BAFTA Longlists Revealed: 'Oppenheimer', 'Barbie' & 'Killers of the Flower Moon' Lead Way as 'Napoleon', 'Ferrari', 'The Killer' Fizzle in Main Categories |last=Ntim |first=Zac |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=5 January 2024 |access-date=5 January 2024 |archive-date=14 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240114081500/https://deadline.com/2024/01/bafta-longlist-oppenheimer-barbie-killers-of-flower-moon-maestro-christopher-nolan-greta-gerwig-martin-scorsese-1235695719/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Ultimately, ''Oppenheimer'' received the most nominations with thirteen, followed by ''Poor Things'' with eleven.<ref name="Nominations"/> The [[fantasy comedy]] ''Barbie'' underperformed, only receiving five nominations, and without nods for [[BAFTA Award for Best Film|Best Film]] and [[BAFTA Award for Best Direction|Best Director]] ([[Greta Gerwig]]).<ref name="Nominations"/><ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2024/01/bafta-awards-nominations-lily-gladstone-martin-scorcese-barbie-snubs-surprises-1235796018/ |title=BAFTA Shocker! Snubs & Surprises include No Lily Gladstone & No Martin Scorsese, Greta Gerwig, or Yorgos Lanthimos in Best Director |last=Wiseman |first=Andreas |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=18 January 2024 |access-date=18 January 2024 |archive-date=18 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240118123500/https://deadline.com/2024/01/bafta-awards-nominations-lily-gladstone-martin-scorcese-barbie-snubs-surprises-1235796018/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/awards/global/barbie-bafta-nominations-best-film-snub-1235875299/ |title=BAFTA on 'Barbie' Best Film Nomination Snub: 'There's No Such Thing as Expected' |last=Ritman |first=Alex |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=18 January 2024 |access-date=18 January 2024 |archive-date=18 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240118131611/https://variety.com/2024/awards/global/barbie-bafta-nominations-best-film-snub-1235875299/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Another notable omission was [[Lily Gladstone]] for [[BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role|Best Actress in a Leading Role]] for her portrayal of [[Mollie Kyle|Mollie Burkhart]] in ''Killers of the Flower Moon''; the "snub" irked many fans, who took to [[social media]] to express their confusion and frustration.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.today.com/popculture/awards/bafta-awards-2024-snubs-surprises-rcna134473 |title=BATFA Awards snubs and surprises: Fans react to Lily Gladstone, Greta Lee, Martin Scorsese |last=Burt |first=Kristian |work=[[Today (American TV program)|Today]] |date=18 January 2024 |access-date=18 January 2024 |archive-date=18 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240118163633/https://www.today.com/popculture/awards/bafta-awards-2024-snubs-surprises-rcna134473 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.gamesradar.com/bafta-snubs-lily-gladstone-andrew-scott/ |title=Film fans react to Lily Gladstone's BAFTA snub: "It's a big loss" |last=Milici |first=Lauren |work=[[GamesRadar+]] |date=18 January 2024 |access-date=18 January 2024 |archive-date=18 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240118185229/https://www.gamesradar.com/bafta-snubs-lily-gladstone-andrew-scott/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.themarysue.com/no-bafta-nomination-for-lily-gladstone-for-killers-of-the-flower-moon-fans-are-confused/ |title=No BAFTA Nomination for Lily Gladstone? Fans Are Confused |last=Pollacco |first=Laura |work=[[Dan Abrams#The Mary Sue|The Mary Sue]] |date=18 January 2024 |access-date=18 January 2024 |archive-date=19 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240119161054/https://www.themarysue.com/no-bafta-nomination-for-lily-gladstone-for-killers-of-the-flower-moon-fans-are-confused/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Additionally, the cultural phenomenon of "[[Barbenheimer]]" received a total of eighteen nominations: five for ''Barbie'' and thirteen for ''Oppenheimer''; the two films competed against each other in three categories.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.standard.co.uk/comment/podcasts/barbenheimer-barbie-oppenheimer-bafta-oscars-movie-awards-b1133554.html |title=BAFTAs and Oscars 2024: 'Barbenheimer' round two? |last=Weeks |first=Jon |work=[[The Standard (London newspaper)|The Standard]] |date=22 January 2024 |access-date=26 January 2024 |archive-date=26 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240126065935/https://www.standard.co.uk/comment/podcasts/barbenheimer-barbie-oppenheimer-bafta-oscars-movie-awards-b1133554.html |url-status=live}}</ref>

English singer and songwriter [[Sophie Ellis-Bextor]] performed her 2001 chart-topper "[[Murder on the Dancefloor]]", most recently known as the concluding [[Viral phenomenon|viral song]] from the five-time BAFTA-nominated film ''[[Saltburn (film)|Saltburn]]''.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/newsbeat-67881277 |title='Saltburn' sends 'Murder on the Dancefloor' back into Top 10 |last=McLaren |first=Bonnie |publisher=[[BBC]] |date=5 January 2024 |access-date=16 February 2024 |archive-date=6 January 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240106234945/https://www.bbc.com/news/newsbeat-67881277 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/music-news/bafta-awards-saltburn-murder-on-the-dancefloor-sophie-ellis-bextor-1235810272/ |title=BAFTA Awards: Sophie Ellis-Bextor to Perform 'Saltburn' Hit |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240131000228/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/music-news/bafta-awards-saltburn-murder-on-the-dancefloor-sophie-ellis-bextor-1235810272/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.bbc.com/news/entertainment-arts-68229594 |title=Will TikTok take 'Saltburn' from Bathtubs to BAFTAs? |last=Taylor |first=Alex |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240218174925/https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/movies/movie-news/bafta-film-awards-performance-murder-on-the-dancefloor-1235828961/ |url-status=live}}</ref> Time]]" during the "In Memoriam" segment.<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a46592013/sophie-ellis-bextor-saltburn-baftas/ |title=Hannah Waddingham to perform at the BAFTAs this |url=https://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/general-news/bafta-film-awards-hannah-waddingham-1235828966/ |title=BAFTA Awards: Hannah Waddingham Performs 'Time After Time' for In Memoriam Segment Waddingham">{{cite web |url=https://www.digitalspy.com/movies/a46844250/hannah-waddingham-bafta-in-memoriam-performance/ |title=Hannah Waddingham leaves BAFTA viewers in tears over In Memoriam BAFTA president [[William, Prince of Wales|Prince William]] attended the ceremony, but, unlike last year, his wife, [[Catherine, Princess of Wales|Catherine]], did not, as she was recovering from [[abdominal surgery]]; he met the category winners and [[BAFTA Rising Star Award]] nominees following the awards ceremony.<ref>{{cite magazine |url=https://people.com/prince-william-attend-2024-baftas-amid-kate-middleton-recovery-sugery-8584054 |title=Prince William to Attend 2024 BAFTAs Solo amid Kate Middleton's Recovery from Abdominal Surgery |last1=Hatcher |first1=Kirsty |last2=Perry |first2=Simon |magazine=[[People (magazine)|People]] |date=16 February 2024 |access-date=16 February 2024 |archive-date=18 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240218134433/https://people.com/prince-william-attend-2024-baftas-amid-kate-middleton-recovery-sugery-8584054 |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/prince-william-bafta-awards-2024-attend-solo-1235914093/ |title=Prince William to Attend BAFTA Awards on Sunday as Kate Middleton Continues to Recover from Surgery |last=Yossman |first=K.J. |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=16 February 2024 |access-date=16 February 2024 |archive-date=16 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240216183030/https://variety.com/2024/film/global/prince-william-bafta-awards-2024-attend-solo-1235914093/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/celebrities/2024/02/18/prince-william-arrives-baftas-alone-princess-kate-surgery-recovery/72652587007/ |title=Prince William attends the BAFTAs solo as Princess Kate continues recovery from surgery |last=Morrow |first=Brendan |work=[[USA Today]] |date=18 February 2024 |access-date=18 February 2024 |archive-date=18 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240218222855/https://www.usatoday.com/story/entertainment/celebrities/2024/02/18/prince-william-arrives-baftas-alone-princess-kate-surgery-recovery/72652587007/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

Additionally, the BAFTA Awards did not stream the final four categories live this year; they instead were broadcast as-live on the [[BBC]] with a two-hour delay along with the rest of the awards. The three-hour ceremony was edited down by an hour to make for a 120-minute runtime. Last year, the show ran with its usual two-hour delay, but the final four categories were broadcast live. BAFTA Awards director Emma Baehr said of the change: "We've tried lots of different things. Last year we went live (...) We're not going to be doing that this year. We tried it. It was good at the time, but it didn't add any more than what we needed."<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/bafta-awards-final-four-categories-live-2024-1235909147/ |title=BAFTA Awards Will Broadcast Ceremony, Including Best Film Category, With Two-Hour Delay |last=Yossman |first=K.J. |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=13 February 2024 |access-date=13 February 2024 |archive-date=13 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240213132710/https://variety.com/2024/film/global/bafta-awards-final-four-categories-live-2024-1235909147/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

While producer [[Emma Thomas]] was accepting the award for Best Film on behalf of ''Oppenheimer'', [[Internet celebrity|social media personality]] Lizwani [[Gate crashing|gatecrashed]] her speech. The cast and crew of ''Oppenheimer'' were seated on the right-hand side of the venue and after presenter [[Michael J. Fox]] announced the winner, Thomas, [[Christopher Nolan]], [[Charles Roven]] and [[Cillian Murphy]] walked together onto the stage to collect the award. As Thomas was handed the award from Fox, Lizwani nonchalantly entered from the opposite side of the venue and ran onstage. Few audience members and viewers noticed him as he silently stood between Murphy and Roven while Thomas gave her acceptance speech. He was soon apprehended by security officials after he walked offstage with the film's team. A BAFTA spokesperson published a statement following the ceremony: "A social media prankster was removed by security last night after joining the winners of the final award on stage — we are taking this very seriously, and don't wish to grant him any publicity by commenting further."<ref>{{cite web |url=https://variety.com/2024/film/global/baftas-best-film-prankster-oppenheimer-crashed-1235916123/ |title=BAFTAs Slam Social Media Prankster Who Crashed 'Oppenheimer's' Best Film Speech: 'We Are Taking This Very Seriously' (Exclusive) |last1=Yossman |first1=K.J. |last2=Ritman |first2=Alex |work=[[Variety (magazine)|Variety]] |date=19 February 2024 |access-date=19 February 2024 |archive-date=19 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240219114030/https://variety.com/2024/film/global/baftas-best-film-prankster-oppenheimer-crashed-1235916123/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/2024/02/19/lizwani-bafta-youtuber-sneaks-on-stage-oppenheimer/ |title=YouTuber gatecrashes BAFTAs stage with 'Oppenheimer' stars – and nobody notices |last=Singh |first=Anita |work=[[The Daily Telegraph|The Telegraph]] |date=19 February 2024 |access-date=19 February 2024 |archive-date=19 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240219151605/https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/2024/02/19/lizwani-bafta-youtuber-sneaks-on-stage-oppenheimer/ |url-status=live}}</ref><ref>{{cite web |url=https://deadline.com/2024/02/bafta-prankster-oppenheimer-best-film-speech-1235830794/ |title=BAFTA Says Security Removed Prankster Who Gatecrashed 'Oppenheimer' Best Film Speech |last=Kanter |first=Jake |work=[[Deadline Hollywood]] |date=19 February 2024 |access-date=19 February 2024 |archive-date=19 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240219131008/https://deadline.com/2024/02/bafta-prankster-oppenheimer-best-film-speech-1235830794/ |url-status=live}}</ref>

==In Memoriam==
The In Memoriam montage was played to the song "[[Time After Time (Cyndi Lauper song)|Time After Time]]", performed by [[Hannah Waddingham]].<ref name="In Memoriam"/><ref name="Hannah Waddingham"/><ref>{{cite AV media |url=https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qTPp4rDKo-E |title=Hannah Waddingham – Time After Time, BAFTA In Memoriam |publisher=HannahWadFans |via=[[YouTube]] |date=18 February 2024 |access-date=18 February 2024 |archive-date=18 February 2024 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20240218230100/https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=qTPp4rDKo-E |url-status=live}}</ref>
